name: scheduled-automation
description: Local scheduler diagnostics, real-runtime proof, safe canaries, correlated run receipts, and missed-run analysis for launchd, systemd timers, cron, and Windows Task Scheduler.

interface.shortDescription: Prove local jobs in their real scheduler runtime.
interface.longDescription: A cross-agent operations plugin for launchd, systemd timers, cron, and Windows Task Scheduler. It separates definition, registration, demand-run, native-trigger, and functional-effect evidence; diagnoses scheduler-owned runtime differences; and supports bounded canaries and correlated atomic receipts without treating a manual shell run as scheduler proof.
capabilities: launchd, systemd timer, cron, and Windows Task Scheduler definition and registration diagnostics
Scheduler-owned executable, working directory, environment, identity, session, permission, and output comparison
Demand-run versus configured native-trigger evidence separation
Fresh scheduler-originated run proof with native event correlation and atomic receipts
Separate production and canary evidence ledgers with bounded cleanup
Missed-run, overlap, retry, catch-up, and idempotency analysis
Rollback-aware scheduler repair with exact-target authority gates
interface.defaultPrompt: Diagnose this local scheduled job and prove the current run through its real operating-system scheduler.
